Permanent Bridge Standard Range

Seven
standard
forms. One uncompromising standard.

A family of pre-engineered permanent bridges from the UK's 2024 Bridge Contractor of the Year — covering pedestrian and vehicle spans from one metre to beyond twenty, designed to UK loading standards, fabricated in our dedicated Wigan facility, delivered concept to completion.

Span &
loading
envelope

Our standard product selector. Each cell indicates the design loading applicable to that product across the span range. Outside the standard envelope, our in-house engineering team delivers bespoke design to order.

1 SSVB and Feakin carry SV80 abnormal vehicle loading on the basis of controlled low-speed crossings (below 10 mph). Beaver Bridges supplies operator signage and guidance as part of the package.
2 Panel Bridges extend to 80 m single span and beyond via multi-span launched configurations. Consult BB Engineering for specific span, loading, and site-constraint combinations.

01

Vehicle Bridges

Four standard forms covering the full range of permanent vehicle crossings — from short-span culverts for rural access, through cassette and half-through forms for minor-road and private use, to full UK highway-specification Panel Bridges for the public network.

Product visualisation — new product, as-built photograph to follow
PRODUCT No. 01 / 07
Vehicle Bridge

Culvert
Bridge

ENGINEERING CODE · BCB

The newest addition to our vehicle range. Short-span, flush-deck, minimal footprint.

Span envelope 1–3 m
0m5m10m15m20m+

The newest addition to the Beaver Bridges vehicle range. A dedicated short-span product for watercourse and ditch crossings, farm and estate access, private driveway upgrades, and drainage culvert replacements. Supported on helical screw piles for rapid installation with minimal excavation or in-situ concrete.

Uniquely in the BB vehicle range, the Culvert Bridge is available with pavement-compatible deck surfacing — allowing the structure to integrate seamlessly into approach carriageways where the bridge is part of a continuous road scheme. The deliberate no-parapet design keeps the bridge visually recessive — ideal for heritage, rural, and ecologically sensitive settings.

Span envelope
1 m – 3 m
Design loading
44 t + SV80
Design life
50 years
Standard widths
3.2 m or 4.5 m
Finish
Hot-dip galvanised
Parapet
None (flush deck)
Typical Applications

Small watercourse and ditch crossings · Farm and estate access tracks · Private drives and field crossings · Drainage culvert upgrades · Rural infrastructure requiring rapid, low-impact installation

Design & Specification

Fully prefabricated galvanised steel construction. Helical screw-pile foundation interface as standard, with Legato precast abutments or mass-concrete pads where ground conditions suit. Default spray-applied anti-skid waterproofing, or supplied with a pavement-compatible deck to receive a continuous bituminous surface. Full 44 t LM1 vehicle envelope with SV80 abnormal vehicle check; no speed restriction.

SSVB on RC bankseat — spray-applied anti-skid deck, Holly Green steel parapet
PRODUCT No. 02 / 07
Vehicle Bridge

Cassette Deck
Bridge

ENGINEERING CODE · SSVB

The workhorse of the vehicle range. Single-lift cassette form, sealed waterproofing, twin-unit scalability.

Span envelope 4–9 m
0m5m10m15m20m+

The workhorse of the Beaver Bridges vehicle range. Welded steel cassette deck units fabricated off-site and installed as a single crane-lift, minimising site time and disruption to watercourses or traffic.

Standard finish is a sealed-soffit cassette with HA Type II equivalent paint in Holly Green — paired with the spray-applied anti-skid waterproofing deck, this gives an integral watertight package without a separate membrane. Full hot-dip galvanising is available via an open-soffit variant where maximum steelwork durability takes priority over sealed-box waterproofing.

Span envelope
4 m – 9 m
Design loading
44 t + SV80¹
Design life
50 years
Standard widths
3.2 m or 4.5 m
Finish
Holly Green paint / Galv
Parapet
Armco / Metal / Timber
Typical Applications

Single-span watercourse and minor-river crossings · Construction haul-road bridges · Permanent private-estate and agricultural access · Small public-road replacement bridges

Design & Specification

Two standard widths as single (3.2 m) or paired side-by-side (4.5 m) units, with bespoke configurations on request. Three parapet systems offered: Armco highway barrier, galvanised or painted metal post-and-rail, or adapted timber post-and-rail. Deck finish default is the spray-applied anti-skid combined waterproofing system; durbar plate is available as an alternative. SV80 abnormal vehicle capability on the basis of controlled crossings below 10 mph; operator signage supplied by BB as part of the package.

Feakin on helical screw piles — shown in client-specified RAL (Holly Green is BB house default)
PRODUCT No. 03 / 07
Vehicle Bridge

Feakin
Half-Through

ENGINEERING CODE · Feakin

Shallow structural zone, wide load envelope, modular variant for hard-to-reach sites.

Span envelope 10–13 m
0m5m10m15m20m+

Medium-span vehicle crossings of rivers, railways, and disused infrastructure corridors. The half-through plate-girder form delivers a shallow structural depth through the deck zone — ideal where soffit clearance is tight or headroom below must be preserved.

Also available as a modular assembly for hard-to-reach sites. Where single-piece crane lifting is not viable — remote upland, restricted-access, or helicopter-lifted installations — the Feakin can be supplied in components and site-assembled, opening up routes and sites closed to conventional precast or single-lift structures.

Span envelope
10 m – 13 m
Design loading
44 t + SV80¹
Design life
50 years
Standard width
4.0 m (with steel kerb)
Finish
Holly Green paint / Galv
Parapet
Metal / Timber
Typical Applications

River, railway, and corridor crossings · Rural and semi-urban public-road bridges · Temporary-works replacement schemes · Remote or restricted-access sites (modular variant)

Design & Specification

Plate-girder main beams either side of the carriageway with cross-girders spanning between them. U-frame action restrains the compression flange laterally without top bracing, keeping the deck open and uncluttered. 4.0 m standard width between parapets featuring a small steel upstand kerb. Metal or adapted timber post-and-rail parapet options. Default HA Type II equivalent paint in Holly Green, full galvanising available. SV80 at controlled <10 mph crossings with BB-supplied operator signage.

Panel Bridge installation showing launched configuration on RC abutments
PRODUCT No. 04 / 07
Vehicle Bridge

Panel
Bridges

ENGINEERING CODE · Panel

Full UK highway specification. Modular, scalable, no speed restriction. Spans from 10 m — upper limits by enquiry.

Span envelope 10 m+
0m5m10m15m20m+

The flagship heavy-duty product of the Beaver Bridges range — and the only BB product designed for unrestricted public highway operation at full UK loading. Full-specification public highway bridges, A-road and B-road crossings, longer-span river and valley crossings, and replacement of life-expired highway structures.

Modular panel system assembled from shop-fabricated, hot-dip galvanised panels bolted into continuous main girders. Configuration flexibility accommodates extensive span and carriageway-width combinations without bespoke design. Single panels side-by-side for heavier loads, double-storey stacked for longer spans, special truss arrangements for the highest loads. Multi-span arrangements use the same panel components as modular steel towers.

Span envelope
10 m and upwards
Design loading
LM2 CS 454 + SV80
Design life
50 years
Carriageway widths
3.15 / 4.20 / 7.35 m
Finish
Hot-dip galvanised
Speed restriction
None — full highway
Typical Applications

Public highway A and B-road bridges · Trunk-road structure replacements · Long-span river and valley crossings · Rapid infrastructure deployment (civil, disaster relief, military-adjacent)

Design & Specification

Standard carriageway widths of 3.15 m (single-lane), 4.20 m (two-lane), and 7.35 m (two-lane with footway). Fast erection by bolt-only site assembly minimising weld inspection and site occupation time. Multiple installation methods supported including crane lift, incremental launching, and cantilever launch over obstacles. All structural components hot-dip galvanised. Chequer/durbar plate deck standard with anti-slip coating available as option. Single spans to 80 m and beyond via multi-span launched configurations — consult BB Engineering for specific span and loading combinations.

02

Footbridges

Three standard forms cover the full pedestrian span range — from the volume BKB kit for private and park applications, through the 2.0 m standard Path Bridge for public active-travel, to the feature Aesthetic Truss Form where architecture leads.

BKB in situ — full galv finish, GFRP deck, horizontal-rail parapet
PRODUCT No. 05 / 07
Footbridge

Beaver Kit
Footbridge

ENGINEERING CODE · BKB

The volume product. Fully galvanised, GFRP decked, maintenance-free 50-year steelwork.

Span envelope 1–13 m
0m5m10m15m20m+

The volume product of the Beaver Bridges footbridge range — a standard, repeatable, pre-engineered kit supplied as a complete package covering structure, deck, parapets, and foundation interface details. Pre-priced standard options streamline tendering and procurement.

Hot-dip galvanised steel with GFRP deck — a deliberate product choice giving low self-weight, inherent slip resistance, no repainting, and a consistent aesthetic, fully aligned with the 50-year design life of the steelwork. The 1.0 m standard clear width suits private, park, estate, and minor-footpath use; wider BKB variants are available from BB Engineering for public active-travel routes requiring LTN 1/20-compliant widths.

Span envelope
1 m – 13 m
Design loading
5 kN/m²
Design life
50 years
Standard width
1.0 m clear
Finish
Hot-dip galvanised
Deck
GFRP only
Typical Applications

Park and estate paths · Golf-course and country-park crossings · School and campus routes · Watercourse and drainage-ditch crossings · Private pedestrian access (public active-travel variant available on request)

Design & Specification

Pre-engineered kit-form construction with standardised components. Helical screw piles, Legato precast abutments, mass-concrete pads, or RC bankseats as foundation options. Galvanised horizontal-rail parapet as standard. GFRP deck only — maintenance-free, inherently slip-resistant, and consistent with the no-paint-cycle approach. Full 5 kN/m² pedestrian live loading with dynamic response assessed to PD 6688-2.

Path Bridge with timber deck and timber post-and-rail parapet options
PRODUCT No. 06 / 07
Footbridge

Path Bridge
Ladder Deck

ENGINEERING CODE · Path

LTN 1/20-compliant pedestrian width as standard. Widens for shared-use active travel.

Span envelope 14–20 m
0m5m10m15m20m+

Medium-span pedestrian and active-travel crossings over rivers, roads, and railways. Urban and park-route feature footbridges, linear-park connectivity projects, heritage scheme replacements. The standard offering where kit-form span range is exceeded but a feature truss form is not architecturally required.

Steel edge-girder ladder-deck arrangement gives a shallow, uncluttered deck profile with clean architectural lines. The 2.0 m standard clear width between parapets meets the LTN 1/20 minimum for pedestrian-only public routes directly — and the product adapts to shared-use widths where cyclist provision is required.

Span envelope
14 m – 20 m
Design loading
5 kN/m²
Design life
50 years²
Standard width
2.0 m clear
Finish
Galv / Holly Green paint
Deck
Timber / Steel / GFRP
Typical Applications

Pedestrian and active-travel crossings over rivers, roads, and railways · Urban feature footbridges · Park and linear-park connectivity · Heritage scheme replacements

Design & Specification

Efficient span-to-self-weight ratio suits locations with lifting constraints or restricted access. Hot-dip galvanised as standard, with HA Type II equivalent paint in Holly Green (any RAL on request) as an option. Metal post-and-rail, timber post-and-rail, or fully bespoke parapets. Deck options across timber boards, steel (solid plate or grating), and GFRP — client choice to suit aesthetic, maintenance, and use-context requirements. Dynamic response assessed to PD 6688-2 for pedestrian comfort.

Aesthetic Truss Form in Holly Green — BB house standard paint colour
PRODUCT No. 07 / 07
Footbridge

Aesthetic
Truss Form

ENGINEERING CODE · Truss

Feature architectural structure. Curved-top-chord Warren geometry — the parapet is the truss.

Span envelope 14–20 m
0m5m10m15m20m+

The catalogue's feature architectural form. Longer-span pedestrian crossings where the structure is part of the visual language — heritage-style and conservation-area footbridges, trail and canal crossings, rail-overline footbridges with architectural requirements.

The Aesthetic Truss Form is BB's branded curved-top-chord Warren arrangement — deliberately designed as an architectural feature rather than a configurable generic truss. The elegant profile, paired with an integrated vertical-baluster parapet forming part of the truss web itself, creates a bridge where structure and architecture are inseparable.

Span envelope
14 m – 20 m
Design loading
5 kN/m²
Design life
50 years²
Standard width
2.0 m clear
Finish
Holly Green paint only
Parapet
Integrated truss web
Typical Applications

Heritage-style and conservation-area footbridges · Trail, canal, and park feature crossings · Rail-overline footbridges with architectural requirements · Projects where architecture leads

Design & Specification

Curved-top-chord Warren truss geometry delivers high stiffness and minimal deck depth, suited to span-to-depth-constrained sites. Integrated steel vertical-baluster parapet forms part of the truss web — one unified architectural expression. HA Type II equivalent paint in Holly Green as standard, any RAL on request. Deck options across full steel (with spray-applied anti-skid waterproofing), timber boards, or GFRP. Prefabricated as a single unit for crane-lift installation. Dynamic response assessed to PD 6688-2.

Interactive tool

Configure your
bridge in minutes.

Answer a handful of questions about your site — span, bank profile, flood level, use context — and get a recommended product, 3D visualisation, and pre-filled enquiry to BB Engineering. Geotechnical and final engineering design confirmed by our team following ground investigation.

The UK's market-leading bridge specialists, delivering over 150 bridges annually. Every project managed entirely in-house — design and engineering in our regional offices, fabrication at our dedicated Wigan facility. Concept to completion, without compromise.
